_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Design and build LLM-based application systems, including system prompt design, tool integration, retrieval pipeline, output validation, and failure mode documentation.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Task AI-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.515), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.509).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.306). Shared vocabulary: [tool]. NICE-only terms [communicate, developers, requirements] vs WIDAI-only terms [application, based, build, design, documentation, failure] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.891, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.043 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Communicate tool requirements to developers" and "Design and build LLM-based application systems, including system prompt design," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.552 > STS 0.306 (gap +0.245). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [tool] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.3%. Shared tokens: [tool]. NICE-only: [communicate, developers, requirements]. WIDAI-only: [application, based, build, design, documentation, failure].

signals.detail: STS 0.306 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.245 with low STS 0.306. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
